name: Chocolate-Gilded Danish Sugar Cones
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Generously grease 4 cookie sheets.
2. Beat butter and sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy. Blend in flour.
3. In a clean, dry bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Blend into the butter mixture with vanilla.
4. Using a measuring teaspoon, place 4 mounds of dough, about 2 inches apart, on each prepared cookie sheet. Spread mounds with the back of a spoon dipped in water to form circles approximately 3 inches in diameter.
5. Bake 1 sheet at a time for 5-6 minutes or until edges are just barely golden. Do not overbake; cookies become crisp too quickly and are difficult to shape.
6. Remove from oven and loosen each cookie with a thin spatula. Shape each into a cone; cones firm as they cool.
7. If needed, return cookies briefly to the oven to soften them for shaping.
8. Melt chocolate in a small bowl over hot (not boiling) water, stirring until smooth.
9. Dip the flared ends of the cooled cookies into melted chocolate and let set.
10. Serve cones standing in a bowl; add about 1 inch of sugar at the bottom if desired to hold them upright.
Makes 16 cookies
